title: Krylov Dynamics and Operator Growth in Time-Dependent Systems via Lie Algebras
authors: András Grabarits, E. Medina-Guerra, Adolfo del Campo
abstract: We study quantum dynamics generated by time-dependent Hamiltonians in Krylov space, the minimal subspace in which the evolution takes place. We establish a direct link between dynamics in the time-dependent Krylov subspace and the underlying Lie-algebraic structure of the Hamiltonian. We develop a general framework in which the dynamics in the time-dependent Krylov subspace is generated by ladder operators of the associated Lie algebra. In particular, we identify the minimal conditions under which the exact time-dependent Krylov dynamics is naturally determined by the interaction-picture Hamiltonian and governed by an embedded $\mathfrak{sl}(2,\mathbb{C})$ subalgebra. We further show that an exact single-exponential representation of the time-evolution operator gives rise to a distinct time-independent Krylov dynamics in a unitarily related basis, from which the exact time-dependent Krylov dynamics can nevertheless be recovered. We also extend the framework to the oscillator algebra as the simplest extension of the nilpotent Heisenberg--Weyl algebra, and provide further examples, including the translated and dilated harmonic oscillator, systems governed by closed Virasoro subalgebras, a spin in a rotating magnetic field, and higher-dimensional generalizations for multi-level systems. In addition, we introduce a new quantum speed limit to the complexity growth rate generated by a time-dependent generator and show that, for evolutions governed by a Lie algebra, it retains the same functional form as in the time-independent case. Remarkably, saturation of this bound is strongly affected by temporal driving and persists only when the Hamiltonian commutes with itself at different times. These results establish a unified framework for characterizing operator growth and Krylov complexity in time-dependent quantum systems with underlying Lie-algebraic structures.
summary: This paper develops a unified mathematical framework to calculate Krylov complexity and operator growth in quantum systems driven by time-dependent Hamiltonians. By leveraging Lie-algebraic structures, the authors provide exact solutions for various models and identify how temporal driving affects the fundamental limits of complexity growth.
main_problem: Extending the Krylov subspace framework and the study of operator growth (Krylov complexity) from time-independent to explicitly time-dependent Hamiltonians.
main_result: The authors establish a unified Lie-algebraic framework that links time-dependent Krylov dynamics to the underlying algebraic structure, providing exact solutions for complexity growth and introducing a new quantum speed limit for time-dependent generators.
method: The study utilizes Lie-algebraic constructions (using ladder operators and root systems), the Wei-Norman factorization method, and the mapping of dynamics to a 1D Lanczos chain/lattice.
model_or_system: The framework applies to quantum systems with underlying Lie-algebraic structures, including su(2) and su(1,1) representations, the Heisenberg-Weyl algebra, the dilated harmonic oscillator, and spin in a rotating magnetic field.
key_observables: Krylov complexity, Krylov occupation probabilities, Lanczos coefficients (on-site and hopping terms), and the quantum speed limit (complexity growth rate).
important_parameters: The algebra type (sigma = +/- 1), lowest-weight eigenvalue (lambda), time-dependent coupling (gamma(t)), and pulse area (Theta).
main_assumptions: The framework specifically targets systems with underlying Lie-algebraic structures and assumes the dynamics can be captured within a defined Krylov subspace.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows probability weight transfer and complexity growth for su(4) pulses; Figure 2 illustrates the real and imaginary parts of the Krylov wavefunction during a frequency quench; Figure 3 displays complexity and occupation probabilities for su(1,1) and su(2) systems.
paper_structure: The paper begins by establishing the link between time-dependent dynamics and Lie algebras, develops the mathematical framework using Wei-Norman factorization and the interaction picture, applies this to specific algebraic models (su(2), su(1,1), Heisenberg-Weyl), and concludes by introducing a new quantum speed limit for complexity growth.
why_interesting: It provides exact analytical tools for understanding how driving and non-commuting Hamiltonians affect operator spreading and complexity, which is crucial for studying non-equilibrium dynamics in many-body systems and driven quantum technologies.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. (a) Krylov occupation probabilities in the su(4) example for T = 1 and Ω0 = 1, showing transfer of weight to the highest Krylov state at long times, with P2 →1. (b) Krylov complexity for T = 0.5, 1, 2 and Ω0 = 1, approaching the corresponding long-time values 1, 2, and 0, respectively.

Figure 2. (a) Real part of the Krylov wavefunction for a representative frequency quench, oscil- lating in the interval 0 < t < τ and freezing to a constant value for t > τ. (b) Imaginary part of the Krylov wavefunction, showing the same qualitative behaviour. (ω0 = 0.6, ω1 = 2, τ = 10)

Figure 3. (a) Krylov complexity as a function of time for several post-quench frequencies ω1 at fixed ω0 = 1. (b) Corresponding Krylov occupation probabilities, displaying the same oscillatory structure (ω0 = 0.6, ω1 = 2, τ = 10).

Figure 4. (a) Krylov occupation probabilities for the su(2) example, with all occupation proba- bilities showing oscillatory behavior for Ω= 1. (b) Krylov complexity exhibiting the corresponding oscillatory characteristics in line with the analytical predictions for Ω= 0.5, 1, 2.
Figure 5. (a) Real part of the Krylov wavefunction exhibiting an exponential decay with additional oscillations. (b) Similar features for the imaginary part (x0 = 0.5, ω = 2).
